retrieved_docs.title: YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure
retrieved_docs.snippet:
YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure

Y Combinator's Winter 2025 Request for Startups highlights AI infrastructure as a top investment thesis. Key areas include: developer tools for agentic AI (debugging, testing, deployment), evaluation frameworks for LLM reliability, multi-modal data pipelines, and cost-optimization layers for large language model inference. YC notes that startups solving agent observability and grounding are especially well-positioned.

retrieved_docs.title: Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025

The Latent Space newsletter surveys the agentic AI platform landscape as of mid-2025. The analysis identifies three dominant approaches: (1) orchestration frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph) focused on multi-agent coordination; (2) RAG-first platforms (LlamaIndex, Vertex AI Search) focused on grounding agents in enterprise data; and (3) evaluation-native platforms (Langfuse, Braintrust, PromptLayer) focused on reliability and measurement. The newsletter argues that evaluation will become the key differentiator as models commoditize.

About
Where enterprise AI meets real-world purpose
Cohere builds foundational models and AI solutions that help teams turn everyday effort into extraordinary impact.
Our Mission
We believe AI’s highest purpose is to enhance human wellbeing. We’re committed to realizing that potential by empowering businesses to scale innovation, boost productivity, and drive progress that reaches everyone.
Our story
From Toronto startup to enterprise AI

Foundations
Cohere was founded in 2019 in Toronto with a bold vision: to do whatever it takes to scale intelligence to serve humanity.
Since then, we’ve remained focused on building enterprise AI that enhances human judgment, accelerates execution, and extends what individuals and teams can achieve on their own.

retrieved_docs.title: Imbue Blog — a-healthy-ecosystem-for-ai-agents-introducing-the-afi
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Imbue Blog — a-healthy-ecosystem-for-ai-agents-introducing-the-afi

At Imbue, we believe that AI agents will radically reshape society in the coming decades. In this future, a healthy ecosystem of open agents and software will be necessary for individuals to have control over the technologies that shape our lives.
Building toward this future motivates our work. It’s why we released Sculptor, with the ambition for it to be the best way to build, modify, and run agents — when the barrier to creating and sharing open agents drops, everyone can benefit from them. It’s why we also work on the conditions that sustain open agents, including business models for open software and fundamental machine learning research to make neural networks understandable.
The future for AI agents isn’t guaranteed. The decisions that we make now, about the things we build (and not build) and the rules that govern them, will shape the world they enter and transform. We need good judgment, humility, and the ability to hold meaningful conversations between builders and policymakers to make these choices wisely.
Last week we joined Anthropic, Intuit, Edgerunner AI, Unstructured, Shift5, and Lilt AI to launch the Agentic Futures Initiative (AFI) to help shape clear, practical, a

retrieved_docs.title: TechCrunch: Agentic AI Funding Surge in Early 2026
retrieved_docs.snippet:
TechCrunch: Agentic AI Funding Surge in Early 2026

TechCrunch reports that agentic AI startups raised over $4B in Q1 2026, with the majority of deals concentrated in orchestration infrastructure, enterprise RAG platforms, and agent evaluation tooling. Notable rounds include CrewAI's $100M Series B, a $60M raise by Composio, and continued growth at Langfuse. Investors cite the shift from single-turn chatbots to persistent, multi-step agents as the key driver of demand.

retrieved_docs.title: Import AI — Import AI 455: Automating AI Research
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Import AI — Import AI 455: Automating AI Research

Import AI 455: Automating AI Research
by Jack Clark
Welcome to Import AI, a newsletter about AI research. Import AI runs on arXiv and feedback from readers. If you’d like to support this, please subscribe.
AI systems are about to start building themselves. What does that mean?
I’m writing this post because when I look at all the publicly available information I reluctantly come to the view that there’s a likely chance (60%+) that no-human-involved AI R&D – an AI system powerful enough that it could plausibly autonomously build its own successor – happens by the end of 2028.
This is a big deal.
I don’t know how to wrap my head around it.
It’s a reluctant view because the implications are so large that I feel dwarfed by them, and I’m not sure society is ready for the kinds of changes implied by achieving automated AI R&D.
I now believe we are living in the time that AI research will be end-to-end automated. If that happens, we will cross a Rubicon into a nearly-impossible-to-forecast future. More on this later.
The purpose of this essay is to enumerate why I think the takeoff towards fully automated AI R&D is happening. I’ll discuss some of the consequences of this, but mostly I expe

retrieved_docs.title: Import AI — Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Import AI — Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting

Import AI 452: Scaling laws for cyberwar; rising tides of AI automation; and a puzzle over gDP forecasting
by Jack Clark
Welcome to Import AI, a newsletter about AI research. Import AI runs on arXiv and feedback from readers. If you’d like to support this, please subscribe.
Uh oh, there’s a scaling war for cyberattacks as well!:
…The smarter the system, the better the ability to cyberattack…
AI safety research organization Lyptus Research has looked at how well AI systems can perform a variety of cyberoffense tasks and found a clear trend of more advanced models being able to do more advanced forms of cyberattack.
“Across frontier models released since 2019, the doubling time is 9.8 months. Restricting to models released since 2024, it steepens to 5.7 months. The most recent frontier models in our study, GPT-5.3 Codex and Opus 4.6, sit above both fitted trendlines, achieving 50% success on tasks taking human experts 3.1h and 3.2h respectively,” they write. “Our most recent open-weight model, GLM-5, lags the closed-source frontier by 5.7 months, suggesting that frontier offensive-cyber capability may diffuse into open-weight form on relatively short timelines.”

retrieved_docs.title: Imbue — about
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Imbue — about

Today, the software and agents in our lives don’t work for us—they work for the company that built them. Closed platforms lock you into their ecosystems, limit what you can do, and serve their incentives over yours.
We're building the alternative: an open agent ecosystem where you can understand, modify, and verify your agents, so they work only in your interest.
We founded Imbue in 2021 to build an AGI future where humans remain at the helm, shaping powerful AI systems rather than being subordinated to them.
We aim to increase human agency in the world, beginning with how we work: with radical openness, freedom, choice, and play. We’re looking for people who build from first principles, question the status quo, and operate with conviction and care.

retrieved_docs.title: Humanloop — humanloop.com
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Humanloop — humanloop.com

We are thrilled to announce that the Humanloop team is joining Anthropic!
From the start, our mission at Humanloop has been to enable the safe and rapid adoption of AI. Now, as the pace of AI progress accelerates, we think Anthropic is the ideal home to amplify our impact.
We've had the privilege of working with customers who continually push the boundaries of AI adoption. We owe a huge debt of gratitude for their trust and invaluable feedback along the way. As we sunset the Humanloop platform, we will continue to work closely with our customers to make their transition as smooth as possible.
We are grateful to our investors: Albion, Index, YC, Local Globe, UCLTF and many fantastic angels. Building this company has been an incredible journey and we were fortunate to have investors who trusted and supported us at every step.
Reflecting on our journey, we're immensely proud of what we built. We were the first development platform for LLM applications and we shaped industry standards for how to manage and evaluate AI. We're excited about this next chapter, joining Anthropic to help build an AI future that benefits everyone.
Raza, Jordan, Peter
and the whole Humanloop team
